title: Cloudflare Scanner
subtitle: Scan Cloudflare Warp endpoints & batch-apply to configs
settings.stopAfter: Stop after N results (0 = scan all)
settings.stopAfterTitle: Stop the scan automatically once this many working endpoints are found (0 = scan everything)
settings.notifyTitle: Show a desktop notification and play a sound when the scan completes
clean.stopAfter: Stop after N responders (0 = scan all)
clean.stopAfterTitle: Stop Phase 1 automatically once this many IPs respond (0 = scan everything)
notify.title: Cloudflare Scanner
endpoint.descFull: Full scan — runs each endpoint through your WireGuard config via xray proxy. Measures latency from the proxy tunnel response.
endpoint.descTCP: TCP check — tests if Cloudflare IP:ports accept connections. Quick connectivity scan, no proxy validation.
clean.useConfigTitle: Disable to scan without VLESS config
clean.descOnePhase: 1-phase — TCP probes Cloudflare IPs for connectivity. Quick test, no proxy validation.
clean.descTwoPhase: 2-phase — TCP probes first, then validates the best results through xray with your VLESS URL for full proxy support.
clean.empty: Configure a scan above and press Start
results.maxLatTitle: Hide results slower than this latency in ms (0 = no limit)
results.empty: Select a config and start scanning
results.notFound: No working endpoints found. Try enabling noise, switching IP version, or increasing scan depth.
results.exportCsvTitle: Download results as a CSV (endpoint, score, latency, loss, QUIC, colo)
results.exportJsonTitle: Download results as JSON for automation
results.tableScoreTitle: Overall quality 0–100 (latency + jitter + packet loss). Higher is better.
results.tableLossTitle: Packet loss measured across several probes of the fastest responders
history.empty: Finished scans will show up here.
apply.desc: Pick a working endpoint, select your configs, and generate modified copies with the new endpoint.
apply.outputLabel: Output folder path (leave empty to save next to .exe)
help.desc: WARP needs a WireGuard config (.conf). Grab one from any source below, then upload it above. These are community tools — pick whichever works in your region.
help.gen1: Configs with license key, multiple formats
help.gen4: Region-specific servers, Telegram auth for extras
help.tg1: 54k+ users, generates WG/AWG configs, region selection
help.cli1: The classic CLI generator, register + generate from terminal
help.cli2: Shell scripts, QR code for mobile
help.cli3: Extracts WARP Connector configs for site-to-site VPN
help.cli4: Bash script for AmneziaWG Warp configs
help.cli5: GitHub repo with free V2ray/Warp configs
help.app1: Open-source, built-in Warp config generator, split tunneling, containers.
help.app2: Lightweight WireGuard client, advanced routing, DNS control, split tunneling.
help.app3: Minimal WireGuard client, battery-friendly, config import/export.
clean.desc: Scan Cloudflare IPs with your VLESS URL and find working proxies.
clean.noResults: No working IPs found. Try increasing scan depth.
clean.coloFilterTitle: Show only results whose colo or location matches (e.g. FRA, Frankfurt)
cleanHelp.intro: Finds Cloudflare edge IPs that are fast and unblocked from your network, then verifies they actually carry your proxy.
cleanHelp.p1: Phase 1 — TCP probe. Quickly dials thousands of Cloudflare IPs on the chosen ports and keeps the fastest responders.
cleanHelp.p2: Phase 2 — proxy validation. Runs the top responders through your VLESS/Trojan config via xray and keeps only the ones that pass a real request.
cleanHelp.p3: 1-phase mode (config off) skips validation — a fast reachability check only.
cleanHelp.p4: Nearby scan expands the /24 (v4) or /64 (v6) around every responder to mine more good IPs from the same block.
cleanHelp.p5: Custom ranges let you scan your own CIDRs / IP ranges instead of the built-in Cloudflare pool.
cleanHelp.tip: Tip: push working IPs straight to the Replacer to rebuild your configs with fresh endpoints.
repHelp.intro: Swaps the server IP:port in configs you already have for fresh clean endpoints — no scanning here, just transform.
repHelp.p1: Proxy configs. Fetch a subscription URL or paste vless:// / trojan:// / vmess:// links, pick the ones to keep, and cross-product them with your new IP:port list into fresh share URLs.
repHelp.p2: WireGuard (.conf). Pick a working WARP endpoint and a set of .conf files; it rewrites the Endpoint line and saves modified copies.
repHelp.p3: Name template sets the label on each generated config — e.g. {remark} @ {ip}.
repHelp.p4: Copy as sub gives a base64 subscription blob you can paste into most clients.
repHelp.tip: Endpoints come from the IP Scanner — use its "Push to Replacer" or "Use" buttons.
clean.copyAllTitle: Copy all working endpoints to clipboard
clean.sourceTitle: Scan the built-in Cloudflare ranges, or your own CIDR/IP ranges
clean.rangesHelp: CIDR (104.16.0.0/13) · range (104.16.0.0-104.16.5.255 or short 104.16.0.0-255) · single IP (104.16.1.1) · IPv6 too · # for comments. Small ranges are scanned in full; large ones are sampled to the scan-depth count. The IP-version selector is ignored in custom mode — exactly the addresses you provide are scanned.
clean.errNoRanges: Enter at least one IP range (or switch to Cloudflare pool)
replacer.desc: Deduplicate configs and replace IP:port with clean endpoints.
replacer.typeProxyTitle: Replace IP:port in VLESS/Trojan/VMess share URLs
replacer.typeWarpTitle: Rewrite the Endpoint line in WireGuard/WARP .conf files
replacer.endpointsDesc: Paste IP:port endpoints (one per line) from Clean IP scan results.
replacer.copySubTitle: Copy a base64 subscription blob (paste into your client as a subscription)
replacer.downloadSubTitle: Download the base64 subscription as a text file
replacer.nameTplTitle: Template for each generated config name
replacer.nameTplHelp: Placeholders: {remark} {ip} {port} {ep} {proto} {n}. Leave empty for the default.
config.fileTitle: Select a WireGuard config file (.conf) to scan
config.chooseTitle: Click to choose a config file
settings.depthTitle: Number of endpoints to generate and test
settings.customTitle: Enter a custom number of endpoints
settings.timeoutTitle: Max time to wait for each probe before giving up (default 6000)
settings.concurrencyTitle: Number of parallel endpoint tests (default: 256 for native handshake, 12 for noise mode)
clean.timeoutTitle: How long to WAIT for each Phase 1 TCP probe before giving up (default 3000). This is not a latency filter — keep it generous so slow-but-reachable IPs are still measured. To only keep fast IPs, use the "Max ms" filter on the results instead.
clean.timeout2Title: Max time per Phase 2 xray validation (SOCKS5 + 204) before giving up (default 5000)
settings.ipTitle: IPv4 only, IPv6 only, or both combined
settings.outCountTitle: Limit results shown (0 = show all successful)
settings.noiseTitle: Send random UDP packets to bypass ISP throttling
buttons.rescanTitle: Repeat last scan with same settings
apply.endpointTitle: Paste an endpoint to apply to configs
apply.configsTitle: Select .conf files to update with the new endpoint
apply.generateTitle: Generate modified config files with the new endpoint
apply.browseFallback: Folder picker not available. Please type the path manually.
results.copyAllTitle: Copy all working endpoints to clipboard
results.downloadTitle: Download endpoints as a text file
clean.vlessTitle: Paste a VLESS share URL for xray validation
clean.depthTitle: Number of Cloudflare IPs to probe
clean.phase2Title: Number of best Phase 1 results to validate through xray
clean.nearbyTitle: After phase 1, scan adjacent IPs near every working endpoint found
clean.ipTitle: IPv4 only, IPv6 only, or both
clean.stopTitle: Stop the running clean IP scan
replacer.urlTitle: Enter a subscription URL to fetch configs from
replacer.fetchTitle: Fetch, parse, and deduplicate the subscription
replacer.pasteTitle: Paste vless:// or trojan:// URLs directly, one per line
replacer.configsSelectTitle: Select configs to use for replacement
replacer.endpointsTitle: Paste IP:port endpoints, one per line
replacer.copyAllTitle: Copy all generated configs to clipboard
replacer.downloadTitle: Download generated configs as a text file
about.desc: Open-source scanner for Cloudflare WARP endpoints and clean IPs.
about.checkUpdateTitle: Compare your version against the latest GitHub release
about.privacy: Runs entirely on your machine — the server binds to 127.0.0.1 only and nothing you scan or paste leaves your computer.
about.donateDesc: If this tool saved you time, a small crypto tip helps keep it maintained. USDT on TRON (TRC-20) has the lowest fees and is the easiest to send.
about.noteTrx: Send TRX or any TRC-20 token. USDT (TRC-20) is the cheapest and most widely used — usually about $1 in fees.
about.noteTon: Send Toncoin (TON) or TON Jettons, including native USDT on TON. Fees are a fraction of a cent.
about.noteEvm: One address for all EVM chains. Send ETH / BNB / MATIC, or tokens like USDT / USDC (ERC-20 or BEP-20). Use BNB Smart Chain, Polygon, or an L2 for low fees. EVM networks only.
about.noteBtc: Bitcoin only — send BTC on the Bitcoin network (native SegWit). Don't send tokens here.
about.credits: Built with xray-core. Thanks to the WARP and v2ray communities.
qr.title: QR Code
